title: How to Use Claude Cowork (Step-by-Step) | AI Agent on Your Computer
summary: Claude Cowork is an AI agent that runs on desktop computers to automate daily tasks by interacting with files, emails, calendar, and other applications. The tutorial demonstrates installation, configuration of connectors for Gmail and Google Calendar, and practical workflows like file organization and task scheduling. It is available only in Claude Pro and Max subscription tiers.
writeup: A walkthrough of Claude's Cowork mode in the desktop app, covering three practical use cases: organizing a messy YouTube topic pipeline file, scheduling recurring automation tasks, and connecting to Gmail and Google Calendar via Cowork connectors to summarize emails and add events. The through-line is that Cowork operates as a local agent that reads and writes files, calls external apps, and can be scheduled to repeat tasks on a calendar cadence, all without manual intervention beyond an initial prompt and approval step.
key_points: Claude Cowork requires a paid Pro or Max plan and runs in the desktop app alongside Chat and Code modes, each suited to distinct workflows.

On a folder of YouTube channel planning files, Cowork found 351 total topics, classified 93 as trending (including Agentic AI, MCP, and RAG) and 258 as evergreen, then wrote a reorganized file while leaving the original untouched.
Cowork supports repeating schedules: after approving the topic-organization task, the demo set it to run on the 1st and 16th of each month at 9 am, with scheduled tasks visible in the left sidebar.
If the desktop app is closed at a scheduled run time, the task executes the next time the app is opened rather than being skipped or queued on a server.

Connectors extend Cowork to external services: the Gmail connector (authored by Google) and Google Calendar connector were added via the plus icon, after which a single prompt caused Cowork to read that day's emails, surface urgent items such as an 11 am meeting, and add the event to Google Calendar automatically.
